summary: Added the first C implementation slice for `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c`, matching 17.48% of an 8.13 KB fighter common file. The new code covers many small fully matched callbacks/stubs for entry, capture Leadead/Likelike, DownReflect, and visual flag setup, while leaving larger functions as `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`. Supporting headers were tightened with concrete prototypes, motion-var fields, item declarations, JObj flag bits, symbol metadata corrections, and a small declaration-sorting helper script.
what_changed: Created `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` with 318 lines of implementation scaffolding and matched small routines for fighter entry state callbacks, capture callbacks, DownReflect callbacks, and `ft_800C80A4`.

Kept many larger or harder functions as `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, including `ftCo_800C61B0`, `ftCo_800C6408`, `ftCo_EntryStart_Phys`, `ftCo_EntryStart_Coll`, `ftCo_800C6B6C`, `ftCo_EntryEnd_*`, capture animation helpers, and several unknown `ftCo_800C....` routines.

Implemented simple callback thunks and direct calls such as `fn_800C63BC` to `ftCommon_8007D7FC`, `fn_800C63E0` to `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, repeated `ft_8008521C` wrappers, capture item cleanup calls through `it_802EADD8`, Likelike handling via `it_802DBA68`, and Leadead position update via `it_8026B294`.
Added common motion variable union fields for `captureleadead`, `entry`, and `capturelikelike`, including `Item_GObj*` storage and an `entry.timer` bool field used by entry animation logic.

Updated `ft_0C31.h` prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders to typed signatures where the new C code gave evidence, including `ftCo_800C6408(ftCo_GObj*)`, `ftCo_800C6B6C(ftCo_GObj*)`, `ftCo_800C703C(ftCo_GObj*)`, `ftCo_800C7A30(ftCo_GObj*)`, and `ft_800C80A4(Fighter*)`.
Updated `ft_0852.h` include guard and declarations, adding the `ft_8008521C(Fighter_GObj*)` prototype and address comments, and changing its includes from `ft/fighter.h` to `ft/types.h`.
Added the new `itlikelike.h` header with `it_802DBA68(Item_GObj*)`, and added `it_802EADD8(Item_GObj*)` to `itleadead.h`.
Added JObj flag definitions `JOBJ_UNK_B18`, `JOBJ_UNK_B19`, and `JOBJ_UNK_B20` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h`, used by `ft_800C80A4` when setting and clearing render-related JObj flags.
Refined `ftCommonData` fields `x6CC` and `x6D0` from `UNK_T` to `int` and `float`, respectively, matching their use in `ftCo_800C703C` as damage/velocity-related values.
Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` metadata by marking `fn_800C77E0` local and correcting `ftCo_804D8DE8` object size from 0x8 to 0x4.
Changed `configure.py` prototype checking option from `--no-require-protos` with `store_false` to `--require-protos` with `store_true`.
Added `tools/sort-declarations.sh`, a small Bash pipeline to normalize declarations, split on semicolons, sort by the first numeric field, de-duplicate, and run `clang-format`.
smart_moves: Matched many tiny callbacks and wrapper functions first, reducing file score quickly while postponing larger control-flow-heavy routines with explicit `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` markers.
Used motion-var union expansion to name per-state scratch fields instead of relying on raw offsets, enabling readable implementations like `fp->mv.co.entry.timer` and `fp->mv.co.captureleadead.x0`.

Improved header prototypes only where the function bodies provided type evidence, replacing broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` signatures with concrete `void`, `bool`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `ftCo_GObj*`, and `Fighter*` parameters.
Marked `fn_800C77E0` as `static` in C and updated symbol scope to local, keeping symbol metadata consistent with implementation visibility.
Added item-specific headers and declarations for Leadead and Likelike calls instead of hiding cross-system dependencies behind generic placeholders.
Introduced missing JObj bit definitions at the baselib layer so the visual flag manipulation in `ft_800C80A4` could be expressed symbolically rather than with only raw bitmasks.
Kept address comments on declarations in item/fighter headers, preserving the projectâ€™s searchable address-to-symbol mapping style.
decomp_lessons: For partially matched files, landing small exact callbacks and trivial state handlers can meaningfully improve progress while leaving large unmatched functions as clear TODOs.
State-specific `Fighter` motion variables should be represented in `ftCommon_MotionVars` as named structs once a file demonstrates how the offsets are used.

Prototype cleanup is a decomp enabler: replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with concrete `ftCo_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, or `Fighter*` signatures makes callsites, callbacks, and headers easier to match later.
When a helper is only used inside the decompiled translation unit, reflect that with both a `static` function and matching local symbol-scope metadata.
Small data-symbol corrections in `symbols.txt`, such as object size and scope, matter because they keep generated or linked metadata aligned with decompiled code.
Cross-system fighter/item interactions often require adding forward declarations or item headers before matching capture-state code.
Rendering-related code should prefer named JObj flag constants once bit positions are known, even if names remain provisional like `JOBJ_UNK_B18`.
naming_conventions: Common fighter state functions use the `ftCo_` prefix and action callback suffixes such as `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Unknown functions retain address-based names such as `ftCo_800C6408`, `ftCo_800C7A30`, and `fn_800C77E0` until behavior-specific names are justified.
Local helper functions may use the `fn_` plus address form and can be marked `static` when scoped to the C file.
Motion variable fields were named by state context: `captureleadead`, `entry`, and `capturelikelike`, with provisional member names like `x0` where exact semantics remain unknown.
Header include guards in this slice use address-style names such as `GALE01_08521C` and `GALE01_2D9A2C`.
Provisional JObj flags were named by bit position as `JOBJ_UNK_B18`, `JOBJ_UNK_B19`, and `JOBJ_UNK_B20`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use empty callback bodies for 4-byte no-op action callbacks, as shown for many `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll` functions that scored 100% in the report.
Implement one-line wrapper callbacks directly when the target call is known, such as `ft_8008521C(gobj)`, `ftCo_80090780(arg0)`, `ft_80084DB0(gobj)`, or item cleanup calls.
For timer-based entry animations, access the motion-var timer field, decrement it, and branch to the next state transition when it reaches zero.
For item-capture state code, store captured item object pointers in motion vars and pass them to item APIs instead of treating them as opaque integers.
For display-object loops, iterate `HSD_DObj->next` while applying `HSD_DObjModifyFlags`, and use named `HSD_JObjSetFlags`/`HSD_JObjClearFlags` masks to match JObj state setup.
When matching collision callbacks, route through existing fighter collision helpers such as `ft_8008403C` with a local callback and `ft_80081DD4` followed by `ftCo_80097D88`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete `ftCo_800C6408`, `ftCo_800C6B6C`, or other `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` functions in `ft_0C31.c`.
Check whether `entry.timer` remained typed as `bool` or was later corrected to an integer/timer type.
Search for later renames of `captureleadead.x0` and `capturelikelike.x0` to more semantic item pointer field names.
Look for later replacement names for `JOBJ_UNK_B18`, `JOBJ_UNK_B19`, and `JOBJ_UNK_B20`.
Verify whether `--require-protos` became the standard configure behavior for future decomp work.
Search for review or follow-up around the address-style include guards `GALE01_08521C` and `GALE01_2D9A2C`.
